content.problemStatement:
Given an integer array AAA, rotate the array to the right by KKK steps, where KKK is non negative.


Input Format

The first line contains two space separated integers, NNN, the size of the array, and KKK, the number of rotations.
The second line contains NNN space separated integers representing the elements of the array AAA.


Output Format

Print the elements of the rotated array, separated by spaces, on a single line.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains two space‑separated integers N (the size of the array) and K (the number of right rotations). The second line contains N space‑separated integers – the elements of the array.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line with N integers – the array after rotating to the right by K steps, separated by a single space.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Rotate right by 3: the last three elements (5,6,7) become the first three.
Right rotation by 2 moves the last two elements to the front.
K equals N, so the array remains unchanged.

solution.approach: For each test case we need to move every element K positions to the right, wrapping around the end of the array. Because K can be larger than N we first replace K by K % N (if N > 0). Then we create a temporary array of size N and place element i of the original array at position (i + K) % N in the temporary array. Finally we output the temporary array. This runs in O(N) time and O(N) extra space per test case. An in‑place solution using three reversals also exists, but the copy method is simpler and fits the constraints.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, total O(T·N).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) auxiliary array per test case.
